title: RBAC Module Permission Schema V1
description: JSON Schema for the permissions block in a Project Lumina domain-physics document. Defines chmod-style octal mode, ownership, and optional extended ACL entries.

mode.description: Three-digit UNIX-style octal permission mode. Each digit is the sum of read (4), write (2), and execute (1) bits for owner, group, and others respectively. Example: '750' = rwxr-x---.

owner.description: Pseudonymous ID of the owning Domain Authority. Must match a domain_authority.pseudonymous_id in the same domain-physics document or in the governance user registry.

group.description: Role name that maps to the group permission bits. Any authenticated user with this role receives the group-level access defined by the middle octal digit.

acl.description: Optional extended ACL entries granting additional access beyond the octal mode. ACL entries can grant but never revoke access already permitted by the mode bits.

role.description: Canonical system role ID receiving the access grant. Use this for system-level role grants.

domain_role.description: Domain-scoped role ID receiving the access grant. Must match a role_id defined in the domain_roles.roles array of this domain-physics document. Use this for domain-level role grants.

access.description: Permission characters granted: any combination of r (read), w (write), x (execute), i (ingest). Example: 'rx' grants read and execute; 'rwi' grants read, write, and ingest.

scope.description: Optional scope qualifier limiting what the access grant applies to. Values are module-defined (e.g., 'evaluation_only', 'ctl_records_only'). When omitted, the grant applies to the full module.
